tool_args.label: MTA Headquarters Procurements - March 2026
tool_args.raw_text: The Procurement Agenda this month includes 6 actions for a proposed estimated expenditure of $345.8M.

payload.title: Metropolitan Transportation Authority MTA Headquarters Procurements
payload.description: MTA Headquarters procurement agenda for March 2026 with 6 actions totaling $345.8M, including noncompetitive and competitive procurements, modifications, and ratifications.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The Procurement Agenda this month includes 6 actions for a proposed estimated expenditure of $345.8M.
MARCH 2026
MTA Headquarters proposes to award Noncompetitive procurements in the following categories:

tool_args.label: MTA Crew Dispatch and Management System (HASTUS) - MNR and LIRR
tool_args.raw_text: Contract award for MTA Crew Dispatch and Management System Implementation, Licensing, and Maintenance for Metro-North Railroad and Long Island Rail Road.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Contract award for MTA Crew Dispatch and Management System Implementation, Licensing, and Maintenance for Metro-North Railroad and Long Island Rail Road.
deliver and implement a new Crew Dispatch and Management System ("CDMS") called HASTUS

tool_args.label: HASTUS Scheduling System Upgrade - NYC Transit & LIRR
tool_args.raw_text: Upgrade of HASTUS Scheduling System: Modification in order to extend the contract for five years to provide licensing and support and add additional funding.

payload.project_name: Upgrade of HASTUS Scheduling System - NYC Transit DOS/DOB/MTABC Extension and LIRR Version Upgrade
payload.description: Five-year extension of GIRO HASTUS licensing and support for NYC Transit DOS, DOB, and MTA Bus Company ($4,191,355) plus upgrade of LIRR HASTUS from version 2014 to 2027 with five years licensing and support ($4,011,613). Total $8,202,968. Co-terminates March 2032.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Upgrade of HASTUS Scheduling System: Modification in order to extend the contract for five years to provide licensing and support and add additional funding.
extend the existing licensing and support of the HASTUS Scheduling system currently being used by NYC Transit Department of Subways, Department of Buses, and the MTA Bus Company
